tool_input.summary:
In Jupiter’s low-altitude main-auroral current system, the **equatorward (more equatorward) main-aurora zone** is associated with an **upward field-aligned current** (from the ionosphere to the magnetosphere; typically carried by **downward-precipitating electrons** that power bright aurora). The **poleward adjacent zone** carries the **downward field-aligned current** (from the magnetosphere into the ionosphere; typically associated with **upward-moving electrons/ionospheric return current** and weaker emission). 

Relevant PDS datasets (Juno instruments commonly used to analyze auroral regions and field-aligned currents/precipitation) are listed below.

candidates.title: ATM Volume jnouvs_2001 (JUNO UVS OBSERVATIONS)
candidates.reasoning: UVS EDRs provide far-UV auroral brightness and morphology needed to identify main-oval zones and relate them to current systems.

candidates.title: ATM Volume jnouvs_3001 (JUNO UVS OBSERVATIONS)
candidates.reasoning: UVS RDRs (higher-level) are commonly used for quantitative main-aurora analyses and comparisons to in situ particle/current signatures.

candidates.title: ATM Volume jnouvs_5001 (JUNO UVS OBSERVATIONS)
candidates.reasoning: UVS derived products can support mapping main-aurora zones and correlating them with current sheet locations.

candidates.title: Juno UVS Calibrated Data collection file
candidates.reasoning: PDS4 calibrated UVS collection for auroral emissions; useful for reproducible analyses with modern PDS4 labels.

candidates.title: JNO J JAD 5 MOMENTS V1.0
candidates.reasoning: JADE moments help diagnose electron/ion populations linked to upward vs downward current regions (e.g., precipitating electrons in upward FAC zones).

candidates.title: JNO J JED 2 EDR V1.0
candidates.reasoning: JEDI EDRs provide energetic particle measurements relevant to auroral acceleration/precipitation associated with FAC systems.

candidates.title: JNO J JED 3 CDR V1.0
candidates.reasoning: JEDI calibrated data are useful for quantitative comparisons of energetic precipitation between the upward- and downward-current auroral zones.

parts.content:
You are a dataset-discovery assistant for the NASA PDS catalog (atm, geo, img, naif, ppi, rms, sbn nodes, ~12k datasets).

Tools are provided via MCP. You MUST call at least one search tool before returning results.

Interpret the user's science question, infer the relevant mission(s) and instrument(s), then search using mission/instrument keywords — not raw science terms alone. Return both PDS4 and PDS3 versions when available. Only return candidates you've seen in tool results — never invent IDs. Stay under ~10 tool calls. If nothing matches, return an empty list.
Which of Jupiter's low-altitude main aurora zones carries upward field-aligned current and which carries downward field-aligned current?

datasets.title: Juno UVS Calibrated Data collection file
